event: Miracle of the Archangel Michael with Saints Florus and Laurus
symbols: archangel with dark wings at center — Michael as commander of the heavenly host and protector
two saints flanking the angel — Florus and Laurus as intercessors and patron saints of horses
horses of various colors below — God's dominion over all creatures and the blessing of livestock
gold background — the uncreated divine light of heaven, eternity beyond time
description: A Russian Orthodox icon on a gold background depicting three standing figures in the upper register: two saints in robes flanking a central winged angel with dark wings, identified as the Archangel Michael. Below them, a dynamic scene shows multiple horses of various colors — white, black, brown, red, and grey — along with smaller human figures who appear to be tending them. The composition follows traditional Byzantine iconographic conventions with frontal poses, flat gold ground, and hierarchical scaling.
theological_description: This icon depicts a popular subject in Russian Orthodox tradition combining the Archangel Michael with the twin martyrs Florus and Laurus, who became patron saints of horses. While Florus and Laurus are post-biblical saints, the Archangel Michael is deeply rooted in Scripture. In Daniel 10:13 and 12:1, Michael is described as the great prince who protects God's people. In Jude 1:9, Michael contends with the devil over the body of Moses. In Revelation 12:7-9, Michael and his angels wage war against the dragon and cast Satan out of heaven. The icon reflects the Orthodox theology of angelic intercession and the belief that God's care extends to all creation, including animals. The horses recall the prophetic visions of Zechariah 1:8 and Revelation 6:1-8 where horses of various colors carry divine messages. The hierarchical composition — angel above, saints in the middle, earthly creatures below — embodies the Orthodox understanding of the cosmic order where heaven touches earth through angelic and saintly mediation.

scripture_refs.reason: Michael the archangel leading the heavenly host in battle is the scriptural foundation for his depiction as heaven's commander
Michael described as the great prince who protects God's people, the basis for his role as guardian in Orthodox iconography
The vision of horses of various colors in Zechariah's prophecy resonates with the multi-colored horses in the icon
Michael the archangel's authority and role as contender against evil powers

_meta.title: Miracle of the Archangel Michael of Flor and Laurus
